metadata.methodology_note: Spatial interaction model using gravitational analogy. Force between counties is proportional to population product and inversely proportional to combined distance^beta. Combined distance = geographic miles (Haversine, normalized) * socioeconomic dissimilarity (Euclidean across 17 normalized datasets). beta=0.1522 -- a low value indicating these 17 datasets are not strongly geographically determined. Clusters reflect socioeconomic similarity more than physical proximity. This is the correct empirical result, not a modeling error. beta calibrated by measuring decay of socioeconomic similarity with combined distance across 250,000 US county pairs. Results at county (FIPS) level. Note: Modifiable Areal Unit Problem applies -- results would differ at census tract level. All 17 datasets weighted equally in socioeconomic distance.
